text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing its external behavior. The goal is to make the code cleaner and easier to understand. When you refactor, you do not add new features or fix bugs; instead, you rearrange the code so that it follows better design principles. This helps reduce technical debt, which is the cost of maintaining messy or poorly organized code. By refactoring regularly, developers can improve readability, making the code simpler for others to work with in the future. In the long run, this saves time and effort.
